ORDER ON (OVERRULING) CROSS-DEFENDANT'S DEMURRER TO CROSS-COMPLAINT

LAW AND MOTION, 302, RULING: THE COURT DID NOT ALLOW ARGUMENT BECAUSE PLAINTIFF'S COUNSEL DID NOT GIVE TIMELY NOTICE THAT HE WILL BE CONTESTING THE TENTATIVE RULING. THE TENTATIVE RULING WAS ADOPTED. CROSS DEFENDANT MIDLAND FUNDING LLC'S DEMURRER TO CROSS COMPLAINT IS OVERRULED. DEFENDANT HAS DEMURRED TO THE ENTIRE COMPLAINT ON THE GROUND THAT IT FAILS TO STATE A CAUSE OF ACTION, BUT HAS NOT ADDRESSED EACH CAUSE OF ACTION. THE DEMURRER FOR UNCERTAINTY IS OVERRULED AS IMPROPER PER CCP 92(C). ORDER SIGNED. JUDGE: MARLA J. MILLER; CLERK: GINA GONZALES; REPORTER: SHERRY SAWYER CSR# 5976.

calendar.matters:
CRC 3.1385 ORDER TO SHOW CAUSE
Off Calendar Oct-24-2017 Dismissal
MINI MINUTES: Alisa Kim, Esq., specially appearing for Plaintiff. Notice having been given and there being no appearance by Cross-Complainant nor an objection filed. The Court orders the balance of the case dismissed pursuant to California Rules of Court 3.1385. Judge: Newton Lam; Clerk: Yolanda Mazariegos; Proceedings not reported. (610)

CRC 3.1385 ORDER TO SHOW CAUSE
Off Calendar Jul-10-2017 Continued To Oct-24-17 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 610

MASTER CALENDAR COURT
Off Calendar Jan-12-2015 Case Settled - Set For Crc 3.1385 Hrg On Jul-25-17 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 610

CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Aug-25-2014 Set For Trial On Jan-12-15 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 206

DEMURRER TO CROSS COMPLAINT
Off Calendar May-01-2014 Overruled
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
Timothy S. Brown, Esq. of Midland Credit Management, Tel. 858.414.0180, for plaintiff/cross defendant and moving party, by Court Call;
No other appearance.

RULING: The Court did not allow argument because plaintiff's counsel did not give timely notice that he will be contesting the tentative ruling. The tentative ruling was adopted. CROSS DEFENDANT MIDLAND FUNDING LLC'S DEMURRER TO CROSS COMPLAINT is overruled. Defendant has demurred to the entire complaint on the ground that it fails to state a cause of action, but has not addressed each cause of action. The demurrer for uncertainty is overruled as improper per CCP 92(c). ORDER SIGNED. Judge: Marla J. Miller; Clerk: Gina Gonzales; Reporter: Sherry Sawyer CSR# 5976.
